name: Bread Cracker
recipeIngredient: 3 cups all-purpose flour (or whole wheat, rye, buckwheat, corn, or oat flour)
1 tablespoon crushed caraway seeds or fennel/cumin
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water.
2. In a medium bowl, blend flour and salt. Make a well in the center of dry ingredients and add vegetable oil and seeds. Add yeast mixture and stir into wet ingredients.
3. Remove dough to a lightly floured board and knead a few times until smooth. Transfer to an oiled bowl, turn dough to grease it, and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F. Punch dough down and cut into 20 pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and press into a disk.
5. Roll one disk of dough as thin as possible by hand or with a pasta machine into a round or oblong shape.
6. Place crackers on a lightly oiled baking sheet, pricking at 2-inch intervals. Bake until lightly browned around the edges, about 15 minutes. Remove to a rack and allow to cool and finish drying. Store in an airtight tin. Repeat with remaining dough balls (makes 20 very large crackers).
